summary: Iteration 27 packet-aligned API aliases: ran /app/backend/tests/test_iter27_packet_aliases.py — 18/18 tests passed. All packet-aligned prefixes respond 200, all HUD-critical original routes still operational, and both /api/teaching and /api/atlas/teach are present in /openapi.json. Additionally spot-checked /api/memory/agents which also returns 200.
critical_code_review_comments: packet_aliases.mount_alias() correctly clones APIRoute handlers (not duplicated logic) so upstream fixes propagate to aliases automatically.
register_packet_aliases() is invoked after all original routers are mounted (server.py L155-157) — correct ordering; alias cloning depends on source_router.routes being populated.

Intelligence status/personas/providers endpoints swallow exceptions and return best-effort payloads — acceptable for introspection but consider surfacing HTTP 503 if persona/llm registries are down for observability.
mount_alias uses `include_in_schema=True` so both original + aliased paths appear in OpenAPI; this is what the teaching alias test relies on.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 27 alias layer (routes/packet_aliases.py) is stable. Original routes (/api/membank, /api/research-sources, /api/research-orch, /api/subjects, /api/transcripts, /api/knowledge/subjects, /api/atlas/teach) all still respond 200. Never POST /api/teaching or /api/atlas/teach in test suites — kicks off long-running LLM jobs. Verify only via /openapi.json + GET->405 probe.
